mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate for equity valuation models, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income expectations, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a monthly industrial production index increase above 0.4% indicates capacity utilization rebounding, which forces equity markets to price in higher near-term corporate output and yields positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative or flat 20-day forward returns during documented post-shock recovery windows, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening expectations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Monthly CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the correlation coefficient exceeds +0.10 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect reverses direction during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing cycle, a monthly housing starts figure exceeding 600,000 units signals renewed construction sector momentum and wealth effect transmission, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 600,000 units during easing cycles consistently precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side contraction and reduced factory throughput, which triggers short-term equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in >65% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient remains negative over a 3-year rolling window.
